questions.prompt: MSLK is selected, but an SLK combined-role shortcut looks convenient. Which actions remain valid?

options.text: Invoke MSLK exactly once and keep all behavior inside MSLK.
Borrow SLK's combined Supervisor/Checker only for one pair.
Reject every SLK role, state, command, and capability transfer.
Switch to SLK when only one pair remains.

questions.prompt: Worker B cannot start until Worker A produces a future artifact. Can A and B launch in one MSLK roster?

options.text: Yes, because their write directories differ.
Yes, if B is labeled waiting_for_dependency.
No; B is not launch-independent and cannot be in the active roster.
Yes, if the Supervisor promises to wake B later.

questions.prompt: Which statements satisfy the MSLK role visibility boundary?

options.text: Supervisor, Checkers, and Workers are visible same-project conversations.
A hidden Worker is valid if its Checker is visible.
Subagents and delegate_task are forbidden.
A background tool call can represent a Checker.

questions.prompt: Order a role's lifecycle when readiness Eval is ready now but its next authorized work is later.

options.text: Create or unarchive the role for Eval.
Complete and record the Eval result.
Archive the role because no next work is ready.
Unarchive the same conversation when its next task is ready.

questions.prompt: Who authors an ordinary Worker's initial GO/CELL plan in MSLK?

options.text: The Supervisor authors every detailed Worker plan.
The paired Checker authors it within Supervisor-approved boundaries.
The Worker plans itself.
An SLK combined role authors it.

questions.prompt: A Worker delivery has a correctable defect. Who repairs it?

options.text: The paired Checker repairs and revalidates it.
The Checker sends a repair task back to the Worker.
The Supervisor routinely repairs all pair-local defects.
A replacement Worker repairs it.

questions.prompt: Select valid MSLK model assignments.

options.text: Supervisor and every Checker: gpt-5.6-sol xhigh.
Checker: gpt-5.5 high when its Worker is simple.
Worker: gpt-5.5 high or gpt-5.6-sol high by task.
Worker: gpt-5.6-sol xhigh.

questions.prompt: A computer cannot reliably run one large CELL, but the GO outcome is necessary. What changes?

options.text: Reduce the GO outcome.
Keep the GO and split smaller device-safe CELLs.
Skip Checker detection layers.
Merge SLK behavior into MSLK.

questions.prompt: Order the gates before the first formal CELLs launch.

options.text: Every exact frozen-roster role passes the 24-question Eval.
Every pair's plan and GO detection profiles are approved.
No-side-effect simulation passes for the identical roster.
Manual MSLK START authorizes all ready pairs in one launch turn.

questions.prompt: Readiness receipts cover roster A/B, but simulation silently replaces pair B with C. Is launch valid?

options.text: Yes, if pair C has similar skills.
Yes, if totals stay unchanged.
No; readiness, simulation, and start must bind the same frozen roster.
Yes, after copying B's receipt.

questions.prompt: Which project progress statements are valid?

options.text: Each Checker reports only its own Worker subtotal.
Every assignment shows project-wide accepted/total.
The accepted numerator never decreases or double-counts a CELL.
Concurrent Checkers may each increment the same accepted CELL.

questions.prompt: Order an evidence-driven supplementary GO for one pair.

options.text: The paired Checker reviews actual accepted evidence.
The Checker appends a versioned proposal without rewriting history.
The Supervisor approves only cross-pair, safety, and Owner boundaries.
The affected pair records GO_REVISION_SIMULATION_PASS before dispatch.

questions.prompt: Order the response when a pair's continuation condition is clearly absent.

options.text: The Checker stops dispatch and reports CONDITION_BLOCKED.
The Supervisor decides whether Owner assistance is required.
The Owner assists or the Supervisor resolves within authority.
The same Checker revalidates all conditions before resuming.

questions.prompt: All pair queues passed, but the optional Goal is not satisfied. What is valid?

options.text: The Supervisor declares completion from queue count.
The Supervisor records GOAL_GAP and affected Checkers design continuations.
Workers design their own continuations.
Convert the active run to SLK.

questions.prompt: A required GO-level detection tool appears irrelevant to one CELL. What is required?

options.text: The Worker may omit it.
The Checker silently substitutes a faster tool.
The Checker redesigns/splits the GO or revises and resimulates before dispatch.
Run it only in the final Supervisor audit.

questions.prompt: A governed Markdown file is about to exceed the context boundary. Select valid actions.

options.text: Split semantically before the append.
Hard-cut an active evidence table at line 1000.
Keep WORK_CONTINUATION_INDEX as a current-state pointer below 200 lines.
Delete detail because the computer is weak.

questions.prompt: The Owner requests a timed MSLK first start before any pair is active. What happens?

options.text: Schedule it and pre-create idle pairs.
Schedule only Checkers.
Convert it to a resume command.
Reject it; complete-roster initial start is manual only.

questions.prompt: After MSLK START, a new independent-looking pair becomes ready. Can it join the active roster?

options.text: Yes, through PAUSE then START.
Yes, if it passes Eval alone.
No; the active roster is frozen and control cannot add or replace a pair.
Yes, if progress denominator increases.

questions.prompt: Select the correct accepted-CELL threshold meanings.

options.text: PAUSE ALL AFTER N uses absolute project accepted count.
PAUSE ALL AFTER N means N additional CELLs.
PAUSE PAIR p AFTER N uses that pair's absolute accepted count.
PAIR and ALL always use the same counter.

questions.prompt: A pause trigger matures while a targeted pair's Worker has an active CELL. Order the safe sequence.

options.text: Mark that pair PAUSE_PENDING and let the Worker finish.
Its paired Checker validates the delivery.
The Checker performs any required repair.
Record PAUSED and archive the pair when no work is ready.

questions.prompt: A paused pair is inconvenient, so replacement Checker/Worker conversations are proposed for resume. What is valid?

options.text: Replace both if pair_id stays the same.
Replace only the Worker.
Reject replacement and wake the same persistent pair after revalidation.
Borrow SLK resume behavior.

questions.prompt: MSLK PAUSE ALL NOW targets pair A in RUNNING and pair B in COMPLETE. What aggregate result is honest?

options.text: SUCCESS because A paused.
PARTIAL with explicit success for A and INVALID_STATE for B.
FAIL and roll A back silently.
Create a replacement for B.

questions.prompt: Which fail-closed command outcomes are valid?

options.text: Unknown pair and missing scope are rejected without state change.
Unknown pair creates a planned pair.
Duplicate command ID returns ALREADY_APPLIED without duplicate dispatch.
A duplicate command gets a new ID silently.

questions.prompt: VERSION says 1.8.2, but README, global mirror, remote main, or annotated tag differs. Is publication complete?

options.text: No; every release identity and content check must align.
Yes; VERSION alone is enough.
Yes; a lightweight tag is enough.
Yes; the global mirror may lag.

questions.prompt: Order the valid MSLK pair boundary when a CELL will edit files and run local checks.

options.text: Verify the canonical assignment workspace exactly matches the paired Worker's bound conversation workspace.
Record WORKER_EXECUTION_GATE_PASS after pre-authorizing every routine allowlisted edit and command.
The paired Checker sends the formal assignment as its final online action.
That Checker enters OFFLINE_WAITING_WORKER_SIGNAL; an unexpected routine approval prompt is WORKER_EXECUTION_FAILURE, not an Owner click task.
